name: Check raw API response
description: Check raw API response
template:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource {{resource_id}} --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null) && curl -s -X PATCH \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"isDisabled": false}' \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/{{uuid}}/_apis/git/repositories/{{repo_id}}?api-version=7.1"
original_command:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource 499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798 --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null) && curl -s -X PATCH \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"isDisabled": false}' \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/e853b87d-318c-4879-bedc-5855f3483b54/_apis/git/repositories/af16575a-e946-4581-8a77-efdeac7bea84?api-version=7.1"

name: Fetch build details via ADO REST API
description: Fetch build details via ADO REST API
template: TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource {{resource_id}} --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null) && curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" "https://office.visualstudio.com/OC/_apis/build/builds/{{build_id}}?api-version=7.1" | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null || curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" "https://office.visualstudio.com/OC/_apis/build/builds/{{build_id_2}}?api-version=7.1" | node -e "process.stdin.on('data',d=>console.log(JSON.stringify(JSON.parse(d),null,2)))"

name: Extract test completion summaries from all bucket logs
description: Extract test completion summaries from all bucket logs
template:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource {{resource_id}} --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)

# Get all Run UI Automation CLI and Retry logs, extract test completion lines
for log_id in 402 406 472 499 507 508 516 524 525; do
  echo "=== LOG $log_id ==="
  curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" "https://office.visualstudio.com/{{uuid}}/_apis/build/builds/{{build_id}}/logs/$log_id" | grep -E "(🏁 Test completed|✅ Successful|test case '.*' passed|test case '.*' failed|Mandatory test case|Retrying test)" | head -20
  echo ""
done
original_command:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource 499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798 --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)

# Get all Run UI Automation CLI and Retry logs, extract test completion lines
for log_id in 402 406 472 499 507 508 516 524 525; do
  echo "=== LOG $log_id ==="
  curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" "https://office.visualstudio.com/e853b87d-318c-4879-bedc-5855f3483b54/_apis/build/builds/46057316/logs/$log_id" | grep -E "(🏁 Test completed|✅ Successful|test case '.*' passed|test case '.*' failed|Mandatory test case|Retrying test)" | head -20
  echo ""
done

name: Extract failure reasoning from all main test logs
description: Extract failure reasoning from all main test logs
template:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource {{resource_id}} --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)

# Get reasoning for key failures
for log_id in 402 406 472 499 507 524 525; do
  echo "=== LOG $log_id: FAILURE REASONS ==="
  curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" "https://office.visualstudio.com/{{uuid}}/_apis/build/builds/{{build_id}}/logs/$log_id" | grep -E "(\"reasoning\".*Failed|test case '.*' (passed|failed)|testStatus.*Failed)" | head -10
  echo ""
done
original_command:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource 499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798 --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)

# Get reasoning for key failures
for log_id in 402 406 472 499 507 524 525; do
  echo "=== LOG $log_id: FAILURE REASONS ==="
  curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" "https://office.visualstudio.com/e853b87d-318c-4879-bedc-5855f3483b54/_apis/build/builds/46057316/logs/$log_id" | grep -E "(\"reasoning\".*Failed|test case '.*' (passed|failed)|testStatus.*Failed)" | head -10
  echo ""
done

name: Create ADO work item
description: Create ADO work item
template:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource {{resource_id}} --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)

# Create a work item (Task type) in the OC project
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json-patch+json" \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/{{uuid}}/_apis/wit/workitems/\$Task?api-version=7.1" \
  -d '[
    {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.Title", "value": "{{value}}"},
    {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.Description", "value": "{{value_2}}"},
    {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.AreaPath", "value": "{{value_3}}"},
    {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority", "value": 1}
  ]' | node -e "const c=[];process.stdin.on('data',d=>c.push(d));process.stdin.on('end',()=>{const r=JSON.parse(Buffer.concat(c));console.log('Work Item ID:', r.id);console.log('URL:', r._links?.html?.href || r.url)})"

parameters.name: value
parameters.default: Framework resilience fixes to reduce Android AI test automation CI failures

parameters.name: value_2
parameters.default:
Build 46057316 (Android AI Assisted Daily Build) failed with 20+ test failures across 13 stages due to 5 systemic framework gaps:

  1. System crash not detected: Pixel Launcher crashes caused 3 wasted retries per test
  2. No per-step retry budget: Stuck UI consumed 60-80 of 100 execution steps
  3. Stale sign-in state: App data not cleared between retries, sign-in tests failed 4/4 times
  4. No FRE/MFA/credential guidance: LLM wasted retries on first-run screens and MFA walls
  5. Fixed session recovery delay: 2s wait insufficient for crashed emulators

This task implements P0+P1 fixes across 8 files (264 lines) to address all 5 categories. Changes are cross-platform (Android + iOS).

parameters.name: value_3
parameters.default: OC\\M365 Copilot Hub Front Door Service

original_command:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource 499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798 --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null) # Create a work item (Task type) in the OC project curl -s -X POST \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json-patch+json" \ "https://office.visualstudio.com/e853b87d-318c-4879-bedc-5855f3483b54/_apis/wit/workitems/\$Task?api-version=7.1" \ -d '[ {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.Title", "value": "Framework resilience fixes to reduce Android AI test automation CI failures"}, {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.Description", "value": "

Build 46057316 (Android AI Assisted Daily Build) failed with 20+ test failures across 13 stages due to 5 systemic framework gaps:

  1. System crash not detected: Pixel Launcher crashes caused 3 wasted retries per test
  2. No per-step retry budget: Stuck UI consumed 60-80 of 100 execution steps
  3. Stale sign-in state: App data not cleared between retries, sign-in tests failed 4/4 times
  4. No FRE/MFA/credential guidance: LLM wasted retries on first-run screens and MFA walls
  5. Fixed session recovery delay: 2s wait insufficient for crashed emulators

This task implements P0+P1 fixes across 8 files (264 lines) to address all 5 categories. Changes are cross-platform (Android + iOS).

"}, {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.AreaPath", "value": "OC\\M365 Copilot Hub Front Door Service"}, {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority", "value": 1} ]' | node -e "const c=[];process.stdin.on('data',d=>c.push(d));process.stdin.on('end',()=>{const r=JSON.parse(Buffer.concat(c));console.log('Work Item ID:', r.id);console.log('URL:', r._links?.html?.href || r.url)})"

name: Create ADO work item with simpler area path
description: Create ADO work item with simpler area path
template:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource {{resource_id}} --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)

# Try with full response to see what happened
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json-patch+json" \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/{{uuid}}/_apis/wit/workitems/\$Task?api-version=7.1" \
  -d '[
    {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.Title", "value": "{{value}}"},
    {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.Description", "value": "{{value_2}}"},
    {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.AreaPath", "value": "OC"},
    {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority", "value": 1}
  ]' 2>&1 | head -30

name: Create PR with linked work item
description: Create PR with linked work item
template:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource {{resource_id}} --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)

# Create PR via ADO REST API
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/{{uuid}}/_apis/git/repositories/{{repo_id}}/pullrequests?api-version=7.1" \
  -d '{
    "sourceRefName": "refs/heads/users/sungoyal/framework-resilience-fixes",
    "targetRefName": "refs/heads/main",
    "title": "{{title}}",
    "description": "{{description}}"Pixel Launcher isn'''t responding" / ANR / "has stopped" errors instead of retrying 3 more times against a crashed emulator
- **Per-step retry budget** (max 10/15 per step): Prevent one stuck step from consuming all 100 execution steps in state-recovery loops
- **DOM change detection**: Detect UI-stuck scenarios when 3 consecutive actions produce no DOM change, and fail fast
- **App data clear on test failure**: New `ClearAppDataAsync` tool (Android `pm clear` + iOS `mobile:clearApp`) called in teardown to remove stale sign-in state between retries
- **FRE/MFA/credential error handling**: Add system prompt guidance for both Android and iOS
- **Progressive session recovery backoff**: Replace fixed 2s delay with 2s/5s/10s/15s + adb reconnect

## Context
Build 46057316 (Android AI Assisted Daily Build) failed with 20+ test failures across 13 stages, wasting ~4 hours of CI time. Root causes: emulator crashes, stale sign-in state, UI-stuck loops, FRE blocking, MFA walls, and session loss.

## Test Plan
- [x] `dotnet build` passes (0 errors)
- [x] Unit tests: 79 passed, 2 failed (pre-existing LLM token failures, same as main)
- [ ] Run Android AI Assisted Daily Build pipeline with this branch
- [ ] Verify mandatory sign-in tests pass on clean emulator
- [ ] Verify FRE screens are dismissed correctly
- [ ] Verify stuck-UI tests fail fast (< 30 steps) instead of consuming 60-80 steps

Linked work item: #11304771",
    "workItemRefs": [{"id": "11304771"}]
  }' | node -e "const c=[];process.stdin.on('data',d=>c.push(d));process.stdin.on('end',()=>{const r=JSON.parse(Buffer.concat(c));console.log('PR ID:', r.pullRequestId);console.log('URL:', r.url);console.log('Web URL:', r.repository ? 'https://office.visualstudio.com/OC/_git/AIHubServices/pullrequest/' + r.pullRequestId : 'N/A');if(r.message) console.log('Error:', r.message)})"

- **FRE/MFA/credential error handling**: Add system prompt guidance for both Android and iOS
- **Progressive session recovery backoff**: Replace fixed 2s delay with 2s/5s/10s/15s + adb reconnect

## Context
Build 46057316 (Android AI Assisted Daily Build) failed with 20+ test failures across 13 stages, wasting ~4 hours of CI time. Root causes: emulator crashes, stale sign-in state, UI-stuck loops, FRE blocking, MFA walls, and session loss.

name: Check if other scripts also differ
description: Check if other scripts also differ
template: for f in graph.js config.js build-embeddings.js stats.js; do echo "--- $f ---"; diff "<USER_HOME>/project-memory/scripts/$f" "<USER_HOME>/.claude/plugins/cache/project-memory-marketplace/project-memory/1.0.0/scripts/$f" > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "SAME" || echo "DIFFERS"; done

name: Reply to all 3 of Sanjay's review comments
description: Reply to all 3 of Sanjay's review comments
template:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource {{resource_id}} --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)
REPO="{{uuid}}"
PROJECT="{{uuid_2}}"
PR=4969737

# Reply to T13 (thread 62162793)
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/${PROJECT}/_apis/git/repositories/${REPO}/pullRequests/${PR}/threads/62162793/comments?api-version=7.1" \
  -d '{"content": "Good catch — fixed in commit 525b97f. `ClearAppData` is now scoped to **sign-in tests only** (`01_TC_SignIn*`). Regular test failures no longer trigger a data clear, so credentials stay intact for subsequent tests in the bucket.\n\nSee `IsMandatorySignInTest()` helper in `AppiumAndroidTestTearDown.cs`.","parentCommentId": 1}' | python3 -c "import sys,json;r=json.load(sys.stdin);print('Reply posted, id:', r.get('id','error'))"

# Reply to T14 (thread 62162821)
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/${PROJECT}/_apis/git/repositories/${REPO}/pullRequests/${PR}/threads/62162821/comments?api-version=7.1" \
  -d '{"content": "Valid concern — addressed in commit 525b97f. We still need the tool registered so teardown can call it programmatically via `CallToolAsync`, but we added a **guard rail** in `AndroidAppiumToolExecutionGuardRails.cs` that blocks LLM invocation:\n\n```csharp\ncase nameof(AppiumLocalToolsService.ClearAppDataAsync):\n    return GuardRailResult.BlockStep(guidance,\n        \"ClearAppDataAsync is an internal tool used by teardown only...\");\n```\n\nSo if the LLM ever picks this tool mid-test, the guard rail intercepts before execution and the LLM sees the block reason. Teardown calls bypass guard rails (they only run in the LLM execution loop).","parentCommentId": 1}' | python3 -c "import sys,json;r=json.load(sys.stdin);print('Reply posted, id:', r.get('id','error'))"

# Reply to T15 (thread 62165260)
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/${PROJECT}/_apis/git/repositories/${REPO}/pullRequests/${PR}/threads/62165260/comments?api-version=7.1" \
  -d '{"content": "The crash message comes from the **LLM reasoning**, not from the Appium driver directly. Here is the flow:\n\n1. The LLM calls `GetPageSourceAsync` and sees the system dialog (e.g., `\"Pixel Launcher isn'\''t responding\"`) in the Android UI hierarchy XML\n2. The LLM writes this into its `reasoning` field: *\"System-level error dialog detected: Pixel Launcher isn'\''t responding\"*\n3. The framework captures this in `testResult.CompletionReasoning` (line 310 of SubstrateLlmAssistedTestExecutionService.cs)\n4. `TestOrchestrator` reads `attemptResult.CompletionReasoning` and checks for crash patterns\n\nAdded a comment in the code explaining this flow (commit 525b97f).","parentCommentId": 1}' | python3 -c "import sys,json;r=json.load(sys.stdin);print('Reply posted, id:', r.get('id','error'))"
original_command:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource 499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798 --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)
REPO="dfbb009c-c5c9-4519-b9b9-3d6fef17d5cb"
PROJECT="e853b87d-318c-4879-bedc-5855f3483b54"
PR=4969737

# Reply to T13 (thread 62162793)
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/${PROJECT}/_apis/git/repositories/${REPO}/pullRequests/${PR}/threads/62162793/comments?api-version=7.1" \
  -d '{"content": "Good catch — fixed in commit 525b97f. `ClearAppData` is now scoped to **sign-in tests only** (`01_TC_SignIn*`). Regular test failures no longer trigger a data clear, so credentials stay intact for subsequent tests in the bucket.\n\nSee `IsMandatorySignInTest()` helper in `AppiumAndroidTestTearDown.cs`.","parentCommentId": 1}' | python3 -c "import sys,json;r=json.load(sys.stdin);print('Reply posted, id:', r.get('id','error'))"

# Reply to T14 (thread 62162821)
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/${PROJECT}/_apis/git/repositories/${REPO}/pullRequests/${PR}/threads/62162821/comments?api-version=7.1" \
  -d '{"content": "Valid concern — addressed in commit 525b97f. We still need the tool registered so teardown can call it programmatically via `CallToolAsync`, but we added a **guard rail** in `AndroidAppiumToolExecutionGuardRails.cs` that blocks LLM invocation:\n\n```csharp\ncase nameof(AppiumLocalToolsService.ClearAppDataAsync):\n    return GuardRailResult.BlockStep(guidance,\n        \"ClearAppDataAsync is an internal tool used by teardown only...\");\n```\n\nSo if the LLM ever picks this tool mid-test, the guard rail intercepts before execution and the LLM sees the block reason. Teardown calls bypass guard rails (they only run in the LLM execution loop).","parentCommentId": 1}' | python3 -c "import sys,json;r=json.load(sys.stdin);print('Reply posted, id:', r.get('id','error'))"

# Reply to T15 (thread 62165260)
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/${PROJECT}/_apis/git/repositories/${REPO}/pullRequests/${PR}/threads/62165260/comments?api-version=7.1" \
  -d '{"content": "The crash message comes from the **LLM reasoning**, not from the Appium driver directly. Here is the flow:\n\n1. The LLM calls `GetPageSourceAsync` and sees the system dialog (e.g., `\"Pixel Launcher isn'\''t responding\"`) in the Android UI hierarchy XML\n2. The LLM writes this into its `reasoning` field: *\"System-level error dialog detected: Pixel Launcher isn'\''t responding\"*\n3. The framework captures this in `testResult.CompletionReasoning` (line 310 of SubstrateLlmAssistedTestExecutionService.cs)\n4. `TestOrchestrator` reads `attemptResult.CompletionReasoning` and checks for crash patterns\n\nAdded a comment in the code explaining this flow (commit 525b97f).","parentCommentId": 1}' | python3 -c "import sys,json;r=json.load(sys.stdin);print('Reply posted, id:', r.get('id','error'))"

If the LLM ever picks ClearAppDataAsync mid-test, the guard rail intercepts before execution and returns BlockStep with a reason. Teardown calls bypass guard rails since they only run in the LLM execution loop (SubstrateLlmAssistedTestExecutionService line 363).","parentCommentId":1}' 2>&1 | python3 -c "import sys,json;r=json.load(sys.stdin);print('T14 reply id:', r.get('id','ERROR:'), r.get('message',''))"

"

name: Fetch all Jagadish and new Sanjay comments
description: Fetch all Jagadish and new Sanjay comments
template:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource {{resource_id}} --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)

curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/{{uuid}}/_apis/git/repositories/{{repo_id}}/pullRequests/{{pullRequest_id}}/threads?api-version=7.1" > "$TEMP/pr_threads3.json"

cat > "$TEMP/parse_reviewers.py" << 'PYEOF'
import json, re, os

with open(os.path.join(os.environ['TEMP'], 'pr_threads3.json'), 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    data = json.load(f)

for i, t in enumerate(data.get('value', [])):
    for ci, comment in enumerate(t.get('comments', [])):
        author = comment.get('author', {}).get('displayName', '')
        # Show comments from Jagadish, and Sanjay comments we haven't seen (id > 1 means replies exist)
        is_jagadish = 'jagadish' in author.lower()
        is_sanjay = 'sanjay' in author.lower()
        is_sunil = 'sunil' in author.lower()
        if not is_jagadish and not is_sanjay:
            continue
        if is_sunil:
            continue
        content = comment.get('content', '')
        plain = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', content)
        plain = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', plain).strip()
        ctx = t.get('threadContext') or {}
        fpath = ctx.get('filePath', 'general')
        line = ''
        if ctx.get('rightFileStart'):
            line = str(ctx['rightFileStart'].get('line', ''))
        status = t.get('status', '')
        tid = t.get('id', '')
        print(f'=== Thread {i+1} (ID:{tid}) Comment {ci+1} | {author} | {fpath} L{line} | Status: {status} ===')
        print(plain[:3000])
        print()
PYEOF

python3 "$TEMP/parse_reviewers.py"
original_command:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource 499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798 --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)

curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/e853b87d-318c-4879-bedc-5855f3483b54/_apis/git/repositories/dfbb009c-c5c9-4519-b9b9-3d6fef17d5cb/pullRequests/4969737/threads?api-version=7.1" > "$TEMP/pr_threads3.json"

cat > "$TEMP/parse_reviewers.py" << 'PYEOF'
import json, re, os

with open(os.path.join(os.environ['TEMP'], 'pr_threads3.json'), 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    data = json.load(f)

for i, t in enumerate(data.get('value', [])):
    for ci, comment in enumerate(t.get('comments', [])):
        author = comment.get('author', {}).get('displayName', '')
        # Show comments from Jagadish, and Sanjay comments we haven't seen (id > 1 means replies exist)
        is_jagadish = 'jagadish' in author.lower()
        is_sanjay = 'sanjay' in author.lower()
        is_sunil = 'sunil' in author.lower()
        if not is_jagadish and not is_sanjay:
            continue
        if is_sunil:
            continue
        content = comment.get('content', '')
        plain = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', content)
        plain = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', plain).strip()
        ctx = t.get('threadContext') or {}
        fpath = ctx.get('filePath', 'general')
        line = ''
        if ctx.get('rightFileStart'):
            line = str(ctx['rightFileStart'].get('line', ''))
        status = t.get('status', '')
        tid = t.get('id', '')
        print(f'=== Thread {i+1} (ID:{tid}) Comment {ci+1} | {author} | {fpath} L{line} | Status: {status} ===')
        print(plain[:3000])
        print()
PYEOF

python3 "$TEMP/parse_reviewers.py"

name: Analyze script quality in scripts.jsonl
description: Analyze script quality in scripts.jsonl
template: node -e "var fs=require('fs');var lines=fs.readFileSync('<USER_HOME>/project-memory/.ai-memory/scripts.jsonl','utf-8').trim().split('\n');var short=0,long=0;lines.forEach(function(l){var j=JSON.parse(l);var t=j.template||j.original_command||'';if(t.length<100){short++;console.log('SHORT:',j.name,'|',t.slice(0,80))}else{long++}});console.log('\nTotal:',lines.length,'Short(<100):',short,'Long(>=100):',long)"
original_command: node -e "var fs=require('fs');var lines=fs.readFileSync('<USER_HOME>/project-memory/.ai-memory/scripts.jsonl','utf-8').trim().split('\n');var short=0,long=0;lines.forEach(function(l){var j=JSON.parse(l);var t=j.template||j.original_command||'';if(t.length<100){short++;console.log('SHORT:',j.name,'|',t.slice(0,80))}else{long++}});console.log('\nTotal:',lines.length,'Short(<100):',short,'Long(>=100):',long)"

name: Check hook registration in settings
description: Check hook registration in settings
template: cat "<USER_HOME>/.claude/settings.json" 2>/dev/null | node -e "var d='';process.stdin.on('data',c=>d+=c);process.stdin.on('end',()=>{var j=JSON.parse(d);console.log('Hooks:',JSON.stringify(j.hooks,null,2)?.slice(0,3000))})" 2>/dev/null || echo "No settings.json"
original_command: cat "<USER_HOME>/.claude/settings.json" 2>/dev/null | node -e "var d='';process.stdin.on('data',c=>d+=c);process.stdin.on('end',()=>{var j=JSON.parse(d);console.log('Hooks:',JSON.stringify(j.hooks,null,2)?.slice(0,3000))})" 2>/dev/null || echo "No settings.json"

name: Check project-level hook settings
description: Check project-level hook settings
template: cat "<USER_HOME>/project-memory/.claude/settings.json" 2>/dev/null | node -e "var d='';process.stdin.on('data',c=>d+=c);process.stdin.on('end',()=>{var j=JSON.parse(d);console.log(JSON.stringify(j.hooks||{},null,2).slice(0,3000))})" 2>/dev/null || echo "No project settings.json"
original_command: cat "<USER_HOME>/project-memory/.claude/settings.json" 2>/dev/null | node -e "var d='';process.stdin.on('data',c=>d+=c);process.stdin.on('end',()=>{var j=JSON.parse(d);console.log(JSON.stringify(j.hooks||{},null,2).slice(0,3000))})" 2>/dev/null || echo "No project settings.json"
